story.iWant: to generate a slide using an existing layout template
story.soThat: brand consistency is guaranteed and generation is fast

tasks.task: Create build-one Workflow Structure 1.1: Create .slide-builder/workflows/build-one/ directory 1.2: Create workflow.yaml with name, description, instructions path 1.3: Create instructions.md skeleton with workflow phases

Implement State Verification (Phase 1) 2.1: Check status.yaml for current mode 2.2: If mode != "single" and single/plan.yaml doesn't exist: error 2.3: If single/plan.yaml exists: load plan and continue

Implement Template Decision (Phase 2) 3.1: Read suggested_template from plan.yaml 3.2: If template name (not "custom"): check template exists 3.3: If template exists: proceed to Template Build (Phase 3A) 3.4: If template missing: log warning and fall back to Custom Build 3.5: If "custom": proceed to Custom Build (Phase 3B - Story 3.3)

Implement Template Build (Phase 3A) 4.1: Load template HTML from templates/layout-{name}.html 4.2: Load theme.json for CSS variables 4.3: Inject theme CSS variables into :root section 4.4: Generate content based on plan.yaml (intent, key_points) 4.5: Ensure all text elements have contenteditable + data-field 4.6: Add auto-save JavaScript for contenteditable elements 4.7: Validate slide dimensions are 1920x1080

Save Slide and Update State (Phase 4) 5.1: Save generated HTML to .slide-builder/single/slide.html 5.2: Update status.yaml (built_count, last_action, history) 5.3: Display slide file path to user 5.4: Offer to open in browser
Register Slash Command 6.1: Verify /sb:build-one command registration exists 6.2: Verify command invokes build workflow 6.3: Ensure command works in single mode

Testing and Validation 7.1: Test with plan.yaml having suggested_template: "layout-flow" 7.2: Test with plan.yaml having suggested_template: "layout-list" 7.3: Verify slide.html created with correct dimensions 7.4: Verify all text elements have contenteditable and data-field 7.5: Verify CSS variables injected from theme.json 7.6: Verify status.yaml shows built_count: 1 7.7: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a single-slide plan exists with a matching template, when the user runs /build-one, then the system loads the matching template from .slide-builder/templates/
The system injects slide content into template placeholders based on plan.yaml
The system applies theme CSS variables from theme.json to the slide
All text elements have contenteditable="true" and data-field attributes
status.yaml is updated with built_count and last_action

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines AC3.2.1-AC3.2.7 for template-based slide generation, including required HTML structure with contenteditable, CSS variables, and 1920x1080 dimensions.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Hybrid Template + Skill Generation pattern - use templates for matching content, invoke frontend-design skill for novel slides. ADR-002.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: All generated slides must follow standard HTML structure with contenteditable attributes and data-field selectors. ADR-003.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: status.yaml tracks mode, current_slide, total_slides, built_count, last_action, history. plan.yaml contains intent and suggested_template.

doc.title: Slide Builder Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: User story and acceptance criteria for template-based slide generation with theme CSS variables and contenteditable attributes.

doc.title: Story 3.1: Single Slide Planning (predecessor)
doc.snippet: Previous story established plan-one workflow, plan.yaml schema, status.yaml patterns, and BMAD XML conventions. Test Gate PASSED.

file.reason: Existing build workflow structure to extend - defines paths, variables, and references to instructions.md
Current build workflow logic - needs enhancement for template-based generation with detailed CSS injection and content mapping
Reference for workflow.yaml structure and template_keywords mapping used in template matching
Reference for 5-phase workflow pattern and BMAD XML conventions to follow
INPUT - contains intent, suggested_template, key_points, audience context for content generation
INPUT - contains colors, typography, shapes for CSS variable injection
INPUT/OUTPUT - tracks mode: "single", must update built_count, last_action, history after build
Reference template showing required HTML structure with CSS variables, 1920x1080 dimensions, and styling patterns
List/agenda template for bullet content - one of 6 available templates
Process flow template for timelines, sequences - one of 6 available templates
Existing command registration - references build workflow, needs verification

npm.package: HTML-to-image conversion for export (Epic 7, not this story)
Google Slides API for export (Epic 7, not this story)

dependencies.note: Story 3.2 does not require npm dependencies - all operations use Claude Code built-in capabilities and file system

interface.name: plan.yaml schema
interface.signature: intent: string suggested_template: "layout-title" | "layout-list" | "layout-flow" | "layout-columns-2" | "layout-columns-3" | "layout-callout" | "layout-code" | "custom" key_points: string[] visual_guidance: string audience: string (optional) tone: "professional" | "bold" | "warm" | "technical"

interface.name: theme.json CSS mapping
interface.signature: colors.primary -> --color-primary colors.secondary -> --color-secondary colors.accent -> --color-accent colors.background.default -> --color-bg-default colors.background.alt -> --color-bg-alt colors.text.heading -> --color-text-heading colors.text.body -> --color-text-body typography.fonts.heading -> --font-heading typography.fonts.body -> --font-body typography.fonts.mono -> --font-mono typography.scale.hero -> --size-hero typography.scale.h1 -> --size-h1 typography.scale.body -> --size-body

interface.name: status.yaml build tracking
interface.signature: mode: "single" | "deck" current_slide: number total_slides: number built_count: number # NEW - add after build last_action: string last_modified: ISO timestamp history: { action: string, timestamp: string }[]

interface.name: HTML slide structure
interface.signature:
...

...

interface.name: Template file mapping
interface.signature: suggested_template -> template file path: - layout-title -> templates/01-title.html - layout-list -> templates/02-agenda.html - layout-flow -> templates/03-flow.html - layout-columns-2 -> templates/04-comparison.html - layout-columns-3 -> (create if needed) - layout-callout -> templates/05-callout.html - layout-code -> templates/06-technical.html

constraints.constraint: Slides MUST render at exactly 1920x1080 pixels (16:9 standard)
All text elements MUST have contenteditable="true" and data-field attributes
Slides MUST use theme CSS variables (--color-primary, --font-heading, etc.)
Slides MUST be self-contained HTML (no external dependencies except Google Fonts)
Template-based slide generation should complete in less than 5 seconds
Auto-save JavaScript must be included for contenteditable persistence
status.yaml must be updated after every build with built_count, last_action, history
Follow BMAD XML workflow pattern with step/action/check/output tags
Reuse plan-one workflow structure for build-one (same directory pattern)

tests.standards: Per architecture test strategy: Unit tests for template loading, CSS variable injection, content generation. Integration tests for full /build-one workflow from plan to saved slide. Cross-browser testing in Chrome, Firefox, Safari to verify 1920x1080 rendering and contenteditable functionality.

locations.location: Frontend Test Gate in story file (manual browser testing)

ideas.idea: Create plan.yaml with suggested_template: "layout-flow", run /build-one, verify templates/03-flow.html loaded
Verify plan.yaml key_points appear in generated slide content
Inspect :root CSS in slide.html for --color-primary matching theme.json colors.primary
Verify file exists at .slide-builder/single/slide.html after build
Open slide in browser, verify 1920x1080 viewport meta and body dimensions
Inspect DOM for all text elements having contenteditable="true" and data-field attributes
Check status.yaml has built_count: 1 and last_action contains template name
